en.text: O great scion of Bharat
O Arjuna, chastiser of the enemy
O Dhanañjaya, conqueror of riches
O son of Kuntī
these four kinds of persons
thing to be known by you
I, as the Supersoul dwelling within his heart
I am the source of manifestation
have been eradicated (by the fortune of association with My pure devotee)
as the supreme
other godly personalities – the demigods – the sun-god, etc.
(3) those whose knowledge is covered
to worship with faith
as the impersonal or formless Brahma
for the strong
as strength of sāttvika quality, or goodness, which enables one to execute his religious duties
worship Me (though their motives may be mixed with action or knowledge)
in the living beings
mobile and immobile beings
of the intelligent
as the intelligence
The worshippers of the demigods
without crossing the universal principles of religion, i.e
is adopted for enjoyment through fruitive actions
is difficult to cross
These four kinds of wrongdoers or unfortunate persons
free from the illusion of duality
by the illusion of duality
and fixed in consciousness of Me
by this manifestation of the three modes of material nature
who am transcendental to these modes
are manifest from these two types of prakṛti, or natures, as kṣetra and kṣetrajña (vide 13.12)
composed of the three modes of material nature
Persons whose intelligence is misled
arising from likes and dislikes
this knowledge of My absolute power (aiśvaryamaya-jñān)
for freedom from disease and death
one seeking scriptural knowledge or knowledge of the soul
the enlightened person (by the fortune of sādhu-saṅga, devotional association)
to that enlightened soul
the purified knower of the soul
you will be able to know
in the form of the individual souls
as the life-span
the action by which the souls are bound to worldly existence
in the ether
without selfishness and attachment
as the desire to procreate
of the entire
Of thousands of men
taking refuge in Me alone (without any attempts of karma, etc.)
to Me, the Supreme Lord
by Me alone, as the Supersoul of the demigod
Me (due to their knowledge being covered by māyā and yogamāyā, My external illusory potency and the internal potency of My will)

by māyā, illusion (despite their study of the scriptures, they consider that only the form of Lord Nārāyaṇ is worshippable, and that Lord Kṛṣṇa, Lord Rāma, and other factual forms of the Supreme are merely mortal)
(1) the karmīs, or fruitive workers
(2) fallen persons, or those who take the path of devotion but later reject it, considering it inadequate or worthless
whose consciousness is completely absorbed in Me
rituals of fasting, etc
by My power of radiance
nature, the marginal potency
even at the time of death
as the origin of the Vedas, Oṁkār
who are performers of virtuous deeds
as the unmixed fragrance
in the earth
by My power as the essential taste
and of the nature of passion
in relation to the worldly plane and to the celestial plane
and in relation to sacrifice
with the knowledge to be realised of My sweetness (mādhurya-anubhav)
in full along with My abode, My opulences, and My associates
as the eternal cause, known as pradhān
at the manifestation of the universe
in all the Vedas
everything, the entire world of moving and stationary beings
all living species, moving and stationary
and he, too, is dear
for self-realisation and Supersoul-realisation
being fortunate influenced by devotion for Me
of the nature of goodness
by particular worldly desires based on likes and dislikes
demigod, representing a form of Me
in the performers of austerities
the power to endure duality, etc
are able to cross
in that particular devotee
of that demigod’s deity
from that deity
with that firm faith
of the bold
and as the prowess
and as the force of radiance and heat in fire
of those unwise worshippers of the demigods
for that form
and of the nature of ignorance
noble, unselfish souls dear to Me
is the best
is of the nature of Vāsudev, Śrī Kṛṣṇa
and of thousands who have attained such realisation
having fully dedicated his heart unto Me
linking your consciousness with Me
According to whichever
and (4) the demoniac (māyāvādīs, the impersonalists, who dismember My form by their arrows of false logic, resembling demons like Jarāsandha)
is as dear to Me as My own self
taking shelter, placing their trust and devotion
as the element of sound
in the moon and sun

ru.text: Ва̄судеве (общее представление о Боге как Личности)
те, чье сознание постоянно связано со Мной
что все исходит из Него и пребывает в Нем
